EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large long period northwest swells associated with the broad circulation of Hurricane Sandy affected the local Atlantic waters and the Caribbean passages. Large breaking waves of 15 to 20 feet produced coastal flooding mainly in low lying and exposed areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large and long period swell flooded calle Marina in the municipality of Aguadilla. Local Schools were dismissed due to the traffic interrumption.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large long period northwest swells associated with the broad circulation of Hurricane Sandy affected the local Atlantic waters and the Caribbean passages. Large breaking waves of 15 to 20 feet produced coastal flooding mainly in low lying and exposed areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large and long period northwest swell flooded the road PR-187 in the Vacia Talega sector in the municipality of Loiza.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A southwest to south low level flow transported occasional fragments of tropical moisture across the local area. This fairly moist flow along with local and diurnal effects was enough to aid in the development of isolated to scattered showers with strong thunderstorms across part of the northwest and north central sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several power lines down in coastal area along Marina road in downtown Aguadilla.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A southwest to south low level flow transported occasional fragments of tropical moisture across the local area. This fairly moist flow along with local and diurnal effects was enough to aid in the development of isolated to scattered showers with strong thunderstorms across part of the northwest and north central sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees and power lines down in Vega Alta blocked routes PR-647, PR-677 and PR-678.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low relative humidity, very dry and stable atmosphere and dry soils enhance fire development across the southern slopes of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several wildfires were reported across the municipalities of Coamo, Santa Isabel, Salinas and Guayama.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low relative humidity, very dry and stable atmosphere and dry soils enhance fire development across the southern slopes of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Significant fire occurred between the municipalities of Guayama and Salinas where approximately 100 acres were burned.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Trough pattern at upper level of the atmoshpere combined with day time heating and orographic effects to produced scattered to numerous showers across north central Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roads 620 and 647 were closed due to flooding along the Cibuco river.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Trough pattern at upper level of the atmoshpere combined with day time heating and orographic effects to produced scattered to numerous showers across north central Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-2 was reported flooded near the Treatment and Diagnosis Center in Vega Alta.  Also, Routes 620 and 690 were also closed due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The dynamic produced by a mid to upper level through north of the region and a jet stream at upper levels maintained sufficient instability across the northeast Caribbean. This, in combination with daytime heating and a moderate to fresh northeast wind flow, enhanced the development of numerous showers with thunderstorms mainly over the southern slopes of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 505 and road 132 were reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Frontal boundary and associated moisture combined with daytime heating and sea breeze convergence to produce scattered to numerous showers across the western interior section of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mudslides were reported on road 111, km 4.6, and km 5.4; road 140, km 14; road 611 in Sabana Grande sector and in road 123 Rio Abajo where several houses were affected by the impassable road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stationary front combined with an upper level trough to our west to produced scattered to numerous showers along the north coast of Puerto Rico. The heaviest rainfall occurred over the municipalities of Vega Alta and Vega Baja.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ojo de Agua, Los Naranjos,Las Carmelitas, R��o Abajo, Villarreal y Sand��n sectors in the municipality of Vega Baja were reported flooded 16 families were evacuated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light winds combined with daytime heating and local effect to produce scattered showers with thunderstorms along the north coast of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-2 between Arecibo and Hatillo was reported flooded and impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large, long period north swells affected the Atlantic coastal waters and the caribbean passages. These swell resulted in rough and hazardous marine conditions between December 23-25. One surfer drowned due to strong rip current and dangerous surf conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 23 years old surfer drowned at Maria beach in Rincon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two vehicles tried to cross a road flooded by the waters of Rio Blanco. Three people were rescued by Emergency Manager officials.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail was reported by the public in the municipality of Humacao.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Small hail was reported at Barrio Guzman Arriba.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed weak trees and branches by wind gusts near and around Urb. Villa Universitaria.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mudslide triggered by heavy rainfall in Urb. El Poli near Road 159 in the municipality of Corozal.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mudslide triggered by heavy rainfall in Barrio Cialitos on Road 146 in the municipality of Ciales.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Portions of Road PR-690 and road 676 were flooded by Quebrada Honda.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surge of low to mid level moisture accompanying an easterly perturbation, combined with daytime heating and local effects to produced scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms mainly across the western interior and west sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported at downtown Mayaguez.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Pulses of northwest to northeast swells affected the Atlantic coastal waters and the Caribbean Passages. Seas were generally less than 7 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 30 year old man drowned at the Vacia Talega beach in the municipality of Loiza.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: North swells affected the Atlantic coastline producing significant breaking wave action between 8 to 12 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An Iowa National Guardsman drowned at Surfer Beach in Aguadilla municipality. A rescue swimmer gave him CPR and the man was taken to a hospistal where he was then declared dead.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nearly stationary frontal boundary northwest of the local region combined with a surface trough to produced scattered to numerous showers with isolated thunderstorms across the northeast section of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 31 was reported flooded and impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fisherman hit by water surge in Jayuya River in Utuado near Road PR-607 and Caonillas Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A subtropical Jet Maxima, combined with an upper level trough and ample moisture at the surface to produce numerous showers with thunderstorms mainly over the central interior and north central sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Extreme flash flooding occured due to Rio La Plata out of its banks. Rio De la Plata flooded also two bridges. One in Road PR-156 at Barrio Higuero where several families were uncommunicated. The other was in the intersection of roads PR-156 and PR-776 in Barrio Rio Hondo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist southeast wind flow combined with strong upper level winds and daytime heating to produce numerous showers over the municipality of Guaynabo. This led to flash flooding affecting several families, roads and local business centers. Radar estimates indicated over 4 inches of rainfall were observed around or over the municipality of Guaynabo during this event. Structural damages, as well as loss of office supplies including computers, photocopies, surgery equipments, drugs and six cars were also damages. Property damage was estimated at more than $60,000.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A total of 50 residents of California street in the Mallorca Urbanization were removed from their homes and taken to Centro de Envejecientes de Mu��oz Rivera as a shelter.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highways PR-3 and PR-53 to and from Ceiba and Fajardo were completely flooded,including the frontage roads.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with ample low level moisture to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms across Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several mudslides were reported across the municipality of San Lorenzo. One family was removed from their house in Barrio Quebrada, Los Davila sector. In Barrio Florida, Los Gomez sector, 10 families were uncommunicated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture from the back side of an upper level trough moved over Puerto Rico and the U.S Virgin Islands. After intense solar heating thunderstorms formed explosively and left up to five inches or rain from northern Trujillo Alto to northern Bayamon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Jose de Diego avenue was closed near Fondo del Seguro del Estado due to flooding.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooded was reported at 65th Infanteria Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms associated with the outer bands of Hurricane Sandy were strong enough to produce moderate to major flooding across the Guayanilla and Caguas area between October 25th and October 27th. Moderate and major flash flooding was reported as high intensity rainfall butted up against the Cordillera Central Mountains of Puerto Rico.|Specifically, moderate flooding was reported on the Rio de La Plata, Rio Grande de Manati, and Rio Caonillas. Major flooding was experienced in southwestern Puerto Rico especially in communities along the Rio Guayanilla and Rio Yauco where approximately 110 families had to be evacuated from their homes because of rising water. While these communities experienced devastating impacts, significant rises and flooding was also reported in many other municipalities around Puerto Rico including the municipalities along the Rio Grande de Anasco, Rio Grande de Arecibo, the upper Rio Grande de Loiza and Rio Espiritu Santo and Rio Fajardo in the northeast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rio Luchetti was reported out of its banks, flooding several streets of Luchetti urbanization.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper trough north of the area combined with a moist east northeast wind flow to produced scattered to numerous showers across the Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rio Fajardo was reported out of its banks over road PR-3.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough across the western Atlantic combined with a very weak steering flow at the surface and daytime heating produced numerous showers with thunderstorms across the Cordillera Central. The hardest hit areas were from the central interior to the north coast affecting the Rio Grande de Arecibo and its tributaries, the Rio Manati, Rio Cibuco, Rio de la Plata, Rio Camuy, Rio Fajardo, Rio Naguabo and Rio Espiritu Santo. A USGS gage in the Rio Tanama basin (tributary to the Rio Grande de Arecibo) had a 24 hour report of over 12 inches on March 29th. The incredible runoff from this rainfall within the Rio Grande de Arecibo basin in addition to flow from  the Rio Grande de Arecibo at Lago Dos Bocas, which rose to almost 303 feet ��� almost 6 feet above the spillway, caused rapid and significant rises in the municipality of Arecibo, downstream from Dos Bocas reservoir to the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: River was out of its banks and flooded downtown area.

The hardest hit areas were from the central interior to the north coast affecting the Rio Grande de Arecibo and its tributaries, the Rio Manati, Rio Cibuco, Rio de la Plata, Rio Camuy, Rio Fajardo, Rio Naguabo and Rio Espiritu Santo. A USGS gage in the Rio Tanama basin (tributary to the Rio Grande de Arecibo) had a 24 hour report of over 12 inches on March 29th. The incredible runoff from this rainfall within the Rio Grande de Arecibo basin in addition to flow from  the Rio Grande de Arecibo at Lago Dos Bocas, which rose to almost 303 feet ��� almost 6 feet above the spillway, caused rapid and significant rises in the municipality of Arecibo, downstream from Dos Bocas reservoir to the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 31 at La Pitina sector was closed due to Rio Blanco out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid to upper level ridge resulted in rapid decreasing in low level moisture.  A couple of significant fires developed in the area around Albergue Olimpico in Salinas which were detected by both GOES and NASA Satellites and confirmed by fire department. Relative humidity dropped to around 45 percent and winds were generally between 10 to 15 knots. Also, maximum temperatures were in the mid 80s which led to easy ignition of fuels.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two significant fires developed around the Albergue Olimpico in Salinas.

Strong gusty winds were also reported with Isaac.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms associated with the outer bands of Hurricane Sandy were strong enough to produce moderate to major flooding across the Guayanilla and Caguas area between October 25th and October 27th. Moderate and major flash flooding was reported as high intensity rainfall butted up against the Cordillera Central Mountains of Puerto Rico.|Specifically, moderate flooding was reported on the Rio de La Plata, Rio Grande de Manati, and Rio Caonillas. Major flooding was experienced in southwestern Puerto Rico especially in communities along the Rio Guayanilla and Rio Yauco where approximately 110 families had to be evacuated from their homes because of rising water. While these communities experienced devastating impacts, significant rises and flooding was also reported in many other municipalities around Puerto Rico including the municipalities along the Rio Grande de Anasco, Rio Grande de Arecibo, the upper Rio Grande de Loiza and Rio Espiritu Santo and Rio Fajardo in the northeast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Public reported Rio Guayanilla out of its banks, entering downtown area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms associated with the outer bands of Hurricane Sandy were strong enough to produce moderate to major flooding across the Guayanilla and Caguas area between October 25th and October 27th. Moderate and major flash flooding was reported as high intensity rainfall butted up against the Cordillera Central Mountains of Puerto Rico.|Specifically, moderate flooding was reported on the Rio de La Plata, Rio Grande de Manati, and Rio Caonillas. Major flooding was experienced in southwestern Puerto Rico especially in communities along the Rio Guayanilla and Rio Yauco where approximately 110 families had to be evacuated from their homes because of rising water. While these communities experienced devastating impacts, significant rises and flooding was also reported in many other municipalities around Puerto Rico including the municipalities along the Rio Grande de Anasco, Rio Grande de Arecibo, the upper Rio Grande de Loiza and Rio Espiritu Santo and Rio Fajardo in the northeast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lago Dos Bocas was reported out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A southwest to south low level flow transported occasional fragments of tropical moisture across the local area. This fairly moist flow along with local and diurnal effects was enough to aid in the development of isolated to scattered showers with strong thunderstorms across part of the northwest and north central sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines were reported down in Barrio Algarrobo.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-2 between Manati and Vega Baja municipalities in front of Padillas Pizzeria was reported flooded.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A fallen tree was reported at road PR-165 between Dorado and Toa Alta municipalities.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several houses were reported flooded at Toa Alta Heights.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep tropical moisture combined with daytime heating and local effects to produced scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms over north central Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy Rain, intense lightning and half inch hail was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak tropical wave passing south of Puerto Rico combined with strong daytime heating and local effects to produce scattered showers with thunderstorms over the interior sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was spotted by several members of the public and subsequent photos were forwarded to the broadcast media and the National Weather Service.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong daytime heating combined with local orographics effects and sea breeze convergence to produced scattered to numerous showers with strong thunderstorms over the western interior and northwest sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At the Cavernas del Rio Camuy park, tarps were reported blown away, seven trees down and 40 people had to be taken into safe area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong daytime heating combined with local orographics effects and sea breeze convergence to produced scattered to numerous showers with strong thunderstorms over the western interior and northwest sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three trees down along road PR-129 in Bayanes sector. Cars were damaged due to trees falling on them.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough combined with strong daytime heating and orographic effects to produce scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms over the south, interior, and southwest sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seven teenagers were rescued at Jurutungo sector, Barrio Real An��n due to surge of water in a creek in the municipality of Ponce.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Isaac tracked south of the area leaving hazardous sea across the Caribbean waters. The outer bands of Isaac left rainfall accumulations of 2 to 4 inches island-wide with isolated amounts between 6 and 8 inches across sectors of eastern and southern Puerto Rico during a four day period. Strong gusty winds were also reported with Isaac.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: River in sector Beatriz in Cayey was reported out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper trough north of the area combined with a moist east northeast wind flow to produced scattered to numerous showers across the Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 31 interception with exit 21 of highway 53 was closed in Barrio Rio Blanco due to river out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper level trough, combined with daytime heating, sea breeze convergence and orographic effects produced scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms mainly over the northwest section of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor flooding affected eight homes in barrio San Antonio due to poor drainage.
